commit.message:
dts: sun9i: increase BCM4330 post-power-on delay to 1000 ms

The BCM4330 WiFi chip on Cubieboard4 sometimes fails to respond to
SDIO commands after a cold power-on sequence.  Symptoms: repeated
'sunxi-mmc 1c10000.mmc: fatal err update clk timeout' messages every
~750 ms after mmc1 initializes, brcmfmac never loads, wlan0 absent.

The 500 ms post-power-on delay in mmc-pwrseq-simple was not always
sufficient to let the chip complete its internal power-on reset before
the SDIO host started clock-update commands.  Doubling it to 1000 ms
gives the BCM4330 more headroom to stabilize, especially on eMMC boots
where U-Boot no longer partially initializes the SDIO controller
(mmc1 is disabled in the U-Boot DTS via patch 0002).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

uboot: fix eMMC mclkreg bug and CLKCR deadlock; add diagnostics

Four-part fix for eMMC boot failure on sun9i A80 (Cubieboard4):

1. patch 0005: Fix get_mclk_offset() to use CONFIG_MACH_SUN9I instead of
   the non-existent CONFIG_MACH_SUN9I_A80. The wrong symbol caused
   priv->mclkreg to point at CCU offset 0x90 (reserved) instead of 0x418
   (sd2_clk_cfg). All mmc_set_mod_clk() calls were silently dropped.

2. patch 0004 (revised): Remove mmc_update_clk(priv) from probe. The call
   used WAIT_PRE_OVER which can hang 2s on a just-reset controller if the
   DATA lines are settling, leaving CMD.START=1 and blocking subsequent
   clock config. Only pre-set CLK_ENABLE so mmc_config_clock's own
   mmc_update_clk(WAIT_PRE_OVER) has a running clock to complete against.

3. patch 0005 (combined): Change mmc_rint_wait() and mmc_blk_probe() error
   paths from debug() to pr_err() so failures are visible at default log level.

4. Update CHANGELOG.md and README.md to document all five root causes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Copilot PR reviewer never submits reviews on dependabot PRs,
causing the entire auto-merge pipeline to stall. Switch to
fastify/github-action-merge-dependabot@v3 which reliably
approves and enables auto-merge (CI still gates via branch
protection). Copilot stays as informational reviewer.

- target: major (no dependabot PR is ever ignored)
- merge-method: rebase (matches existing convention)

commit.message:
Fix #60: resolve $ref in response headers pointing to #/components/headers/ (#72)

Response header definitions using $ref (e.g. $ref: '#/components/headers/foo')
were passed to Jinja templates unresolved, causing an UndefinedError when the
template accessed .schema or .description.

Added get_response_headers() method to OpenAPIV3DocumentationHandler that
calls _resolve_opt_ref() on each header value before returning the dict.
Updated both mkdocs and markdown request-responses templates to call this
method instead of iterating definition.headers directly.

Added regression test with a fixture (example9-openapi.yaml) covering
both $ref headers and inline headers in the same response.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: resolve cycle-6 review issues (double-quoting, UTF-8, checkpoint escaping)

- Remove extra quotes around json_escape() callsites in computer.rs, agent.rs,
  stealth.rs (9 sites) — json_escape() already returns quoted strings via
  serde_json::to_string(), so wrapping in "..." produced invalid JS like
  ""body""

- Replace chars().enumerate() with char_indices() in task_planner.rs to prevent
  panic on non-ASCII input — enumerate yields character indices, not byte offsets

- Use serde_json::to_string() for localStorage/sessionStorage key/value escaping
  in harness.rs checkpoint restore — handles newlines, unicode, and all special
  characters correctly (matching web_storage.rs pattern)

commit.message:
Fix profile picture upload hang + add timeouts and error feedback (#21)

* feat: US-001 - Diagnose and fix the root cause of the upload hang

Root cause: admin.storage().bucket() was called without a bucket name and
initializeApp() had no storageBucket config, causing storage operations to
hang indefinitely on an invalid bucket URL.

Fixes:
- Add storageBucket to all initializeApp() calls (uses FIREBASE_STORAGE_BUCKET
  env var with {projectId}.appspot.com default)
- Add try/catch error handling around anime filter, storage save, and signed
  URL generation in upload route — returns specific error responses instead
  of hanging
- Add pino logging for upload start/complete/error events with uid and file size
- Parallelize Sobel X/Y edge detection in anime filter (Promise.all) for
  ~2x speedup on the heaviest processing step

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: US-002 - Add explicit Firebase Storage bucket configuration

- Create lib/storage.ts with getStorageBucket() helper that reads
  FIREBASE_STORAGE_BUCKET env var (falls back to {projectId}.appspot.com)
- Add checkStorageConnectivity() startup check that logs warning if
  bucket is unreachable (does not block startup)
- Replace direct admin.storage().bucket() calls in users.ts with
  getStorageBucket() for explicit bucket configuration
- Add FIREBASE_STORAGE_BUCKET secret + env var to Bicep template
- Fetch and set FIREBASE_STORAGE_BUCKET in deploy-api.yml for both
  beta and production environments
- Document FIREBASE_STORAGE_BUCKET in CLAUDE.md
- Update test mocks to use new lib/storage module

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: US-003 - Add server-side processing timeout for anime filter

Wrap animeFilter() call with Promise.race against a 15-second timeout.
On timeout, respond with 500 and a user-friendly error message.
Log timeout events at error level with UID and file size.
Add unit tests for timeout and generic error behavior.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: US-004 - Add client-side upload timeout for web

Add AbortController with 20-second timeout to apiUploadFile in
apps/web/src/lib/api.ts. When timeout fires, the fetch is aborted and
a user-friendly error message is thrown. The account page's existing
uploadError state displays the message, and the uploading spinner is
cleared by the finally block.

Also supports an optional external AbortSignal for caller-controlled
cancellation (e.g., Cancel button in future US-005).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: US-005 - Improve upload error feedback in web UI

- Dynamic status text: changes from 'Anime-fying your photo…' to 'Still working…' after 5 seconds
- Cancel button appears after 5 seconds, allows aborting in-progress uploads
- Retry button shown alongside error messages for re-uploading last file
- Specific error messages for timeout, file too large, invalid type, server error, network failure

commit.message:
Per-body force-based damping, solver stability, skinning fixes

Physics damping:
- Add linear_damping/angular_damping fields to phys_body_t (88→96 bytes)
- Force-based damping: F=-c*v (linear), τ=-c*ω (angular) through
  mass and inertia tensor respectively — heavier bodies resist more
- Y-component linear damping at 10% of XZ to allow natural free-fall
- Volume-scaled coefficients for ragdoll bodies: cbrt(vol/ref) scaling
  approximates cross-section differences between body parts
- Wired into tick_parallel.c (sub-substep), integrate.c, integrate_par.c
- inv_inertia_world passed to integrate args for angular damping

Solver stability:
- PHYS_ROW_FLAG_ANGULAR on all angular constraint rows
- Compliance regularization on cone-twist (α=1e-2) and hinge (α=1e-3)
- Interleaved two-pass solve: animation constraints first, structural second
- Animation constraint softness (50% effective mass for is_joint==1)
- Angular Baumgarte at 5% for flagged rows
- All fixes ported to parallel solver (tgs_solve_par.c)

Skinning fixes:
- FVMA exporter forces armature to rest pose before mesh evaluation
  (fixes mesh baked in posed state vs rest-pose IBMs)
- Bone weight normalization at engine load time in skeletal_mesh_fvma.c
  (Blender raw weights don't sum to 1.0; shader requires it)

* refactor: address review feedback on pickReadableForeground

Co-authored-by: Anselmoo <13209783+Anselmoo@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: guarantee WCAG AA on buttons by falling back to #000000/#ffffff

pickReadableForeground previously selected the best of two palette
colors but could not guarantee WCAG AA (4.5:1) when mid-luminance
accent backgrounds (e.g. teal #0085a7) left both fg0 (~2.81) and
bg0 (~4.37) below the threshold.

The updated logic:
1. Evaluates all palette candidates as before.
2. Returns the highest-contrast palette color when it meets 4.5:1.
3. When neither palette option reaches 4.5:1, falls back to whichever
   of pure black (#000000) or pure white (#ffffff) scores higher —
   which is mathematically guaranteed to achieve WCAG AA for any
   background luminance.

- Updated mid-luminance test to assert >=4.5 (was 'best of two')
- Added contrast.test.ts with unit coverage for pickReadableForeground
  including the AA-guarantee invariant across 10 background values
